page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server instance featuring 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 180 GB of VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores, 2 threads per core) operating at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. It includes 384.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results show a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU (1201.38) and Geekbench single-core (1072.0), fall into the bottom 25% tier. However, the multi-core and parallel processing capabilities are strong, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance reaching 75,745.7 and a Passmark CPU score of 49,105.08, both placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also a strong point, with read-only small block cached bandwidth measured at 4,211.44 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 94.37 GB/sec.

The integration of 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs enables top-tier performance in large language model (LLM) inference. Prompt processing speeds for small (135M), medium (7B), and large (70B) models rank in the top 10% tier, achieving 11,350.23, 2,692.08, and 294.28 tokens per second respectively. Text generation speeds also show strong results, with the medium (7B) model generating 52.68 tokens per second (top 10%) and the large (70B) model generating 5.77 tokens per second (top 25%).

The primary tradeoff of the g2-standard-96 is its relatively weak single-threaded execution speed, as evidenced by lower scores in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of GPU accelerators and dedicated vCPUs makes it a specialized resource for parallelized workloads rather than general single-threaded tasks. The instance is suited for large-scale LLM inference, multi-threaded scientific computing, parallel database operations, and GPU-accelerated rendering or machine learning workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 180 GB of total VRAM, paired with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (48 physical cores) and 384.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.2 GHz, this instance lacks local storage. While single-core benchmarks like Geekbench single-core (1072.0) are in the bottom 25%, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are strong, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 75,745.7. The server excels in LLM inference, achieving top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models. This hardware profile presents a tradeoff between weak single-threaded performance and exceptional parallel processing. It is suited for GPU-accelerated machine learning, LLM inference, and multi-threaded compute workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server with 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 384 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference and parallel computing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 features 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, 96 vCPUs, and 384 GB RAM, optimized for parallel GPU workloads and LLM inference.
tagline: A graphics-optimized platform combining eight hardware accelerators with ninety-six dedicated virtual cores for highly parallelized machine learning and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs providing 180 GB of total VRAM
Ninety-six dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 384 GB of system memory
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small, medium, and large LLMs
Strong multi-core CPU and high-bandwidth memory performance
Tradeoff of lower single-threaded processing speeds
Suited for machine learning inference, parallel databases, and scientific computing
